Picodash

Active graph: packages/panel + apps/web + apps/website.

Project status: public preview; Issues are available for feedback, and pull requests are currently disabled.

Package import surface:
- picodash
- @picodash/panel/advanced
- @picodash/panel/ui
- @picodash/panel/style.css

What it provides:
- application-owned panel stores via createPicodashPanelStore
- PicodashProvider, PicodashPanel, PicodashItem, built-in inputs and charts
- centralized shadcn aria-rhea primitives via @picodash/panel/ui
- provider-scoped panel visibility, activation, reactive placement, and runtime setPlacement via usePicodashPanel
- floating, magnetic, and fixed panel placement; magnetic edges use PicodashPanelSnapPosition
- viewport-default geometry with provider panelBoundary and per-panel boundary Element/ref overrides
- collapsible fixed sidebars with pinned start/end lanes and a scroll-faded auto lane
- explicit application-owned reads via usePicodashPanelStoreSelector(store, selector)
- advanced provider access via usePicodashProviderSelector and usePicodashProviderStoreApi
- contextual advanced panel access via usePicodashPanelSelector and usePicodashPanelStoreApi
- optional panel close buttons with hide or deregister behavior
- strict programmatic writes, sync validation, JSON-compatible values
- pointer and keyboard item reordering with same-band constraints
- observable async sparkline source failures
- theme/portal aware styling
- lazy Recharts and react-dropzone implementations behind stable public components
- import/export and repair flows

Workspace pages (canonical `apps/web`):
- /, /gallery (compatibility redirect), /store, /usage, /more-examples
- /state-lab/provider, /state-lab/scene, /state-lab/built-in-items, /state-lab/custom-items (debugging-only)
- /panel-geometry-lab (debugging-only)
- /state-lab (legacy canonical alias in path-mapped source; debug surface only)

`/demo` is deprecated legacy and not an active route or API here.

Legacy `apps/website` route subset:
- /, /gallery (compatibility redirect)

App surface mapping:
- apps/web: Next.js route-based source app
- apps/website: legacy Vite source app with a reduced route set

Important migration:
- The old schema-driven registration API and old persistence are retired; map legacy `panel` specifiers to `@picodash/panel` imports.
- no compatibility facade is provided.
- Gearmo is a known breaking consumer requiring separate migration planning.

Ports:
- 6030 website dev
- 6031 website preview
- 6035 this worktree via WEBSITE_PORT
- remaining 6032-6034 and 6036-6039 available

Docs to keep aligned:
- README.md
- packages/panel/README.md
- CONTRIBUTING.md
- SECURITY.md
- RELEASING.md
- SKILL.md
- AGENTS.md
- llms.txt

Verification:
- vp run @picodash/panel#build before workspace-wide checks or builds
- bun audit --audit-level=high
- bun run --cwd packages/panel release:check
- bun run ready
- bun run web
- bun run --filter @picodash/web check
- bun run --filter @picodash/web test:e2e
- bun run website
- GitHub CI runs parallel quality and E2E jobs for pull requests and main pushes
